posts.text:
Our office attended the Southeast & Caribbean Disaster Resilience Partnership's 10th Anniversary Annual Meeting, bringing together practitioners and stakeholders from across sectors to build smarter, stronger disaster preparedness for our communities.

From emergency response to long-term recovery, proactive collaboration is how we protect state, territorial, tribal, and local communities before disaster ever strikes.

The Lowcountry knows emergency preparedness better than anyone.

South Caroli

Every victim deserves justice. Every crime deserves prosecution. But the reality is the rich and powerful rarely face consequences.

This is exactly why the Epstein victims must be louder than ever. We will not let this go away. We will keep pushing and demanding answers until the truth can no longer be ignored.

Video

LOWCOUNTRY UPDATE
Weekly Report from Congresswoman Nancy Mace

CONSTITUENT SERVICES
Our casework team is managing 600 active cases and has now returned $38,297,686 directly to constituents owed money by the federal government.

This week, our casework team also received a call, a resident of SC-01 was among 40 Americans stranded in Israel. Our team stepped in and helped get them home. 

IN THE DISTRICT
Our team completed 14 events, meetings, ribbon cuttings, and site visits across the Lowcountry

We are keeping White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and her family in our prayers following her breast cancer diagnosis.

May God watch over her and give her strength as she begins treatment in the days ahead. 🙏

❗REMINDER❗ 

The deadline for South Carolina's 1st District high school students to submit their work for the Congressional Art Competition is Monday, March 30th.

The winner will have their art displayed in the United States Capitol and earn a chance to visit our Congressional office in Washington, D.C. 

Learn more here: mace.house.gov/2026-congress…

We have not forgotten the survivors of Jeffrey Epstein, and we will never stop working to get them the justice they deserve.

Release the files. All of them this time.

We are working alongside @GreyBullRescue to keep the focus exactly where it belongs: on every American still stranded in the Middle East waiting for a way home. 

We will not stop, we will not slow down, and we will not rest until every one of our people is safely back in the United States.

MACE WIN: The National Police Association has endorsed our bill, the Lawless Cities Accountability Act (H.R. 3920).

"The Lawless Cities Accountability Act of 2025 will ensure that administrations failing to uphold the law will not be rewarded with our hard-earned tax dollars," said Paula Fitzsimmons, Legislative Director, National Police Association.

We stand with law enforcement. When cities refuse to enforce the law, defund police departments, or tie officers' hands while crime skyrockets, t

No U.S. troops in Iran.
Community note: Honorable Representative Nancy Mace voted NAY "without hesitation" on the War Powers resolution. She insists "The Iranian regime will fail."

"Rep. Nancy Mace Issues Statement On War Powers Vote"
mace.house.gov/media/press-re…

congress.gov/bill/119th-con…

nancymace.org/operation-epic…

Our office attended the 4th Annual Burger Bash for Developmental Disabilities to celebrate National Developmental Disabilities Awareness Month. 

The event helped raise money for SOAR, Programs for Exceptional People and Pockets Full of Sunshine to support their organizations' work serving individuals with disabilities. 

South Carolina STRONG.

God blessed us with the opportunity to take part in this rescue mission in the Middle East.

Video

🚨 School Schedule Changes in the Lowcountry 🚨

Ahead of expected severe weather tomorrow, some Lowcountry schools have announced schedule changes, including cancellations and e-learning days.

Be sure to stay up to date with your district and local weather information.

South Carolina STRONG.

Let us put this in perspective.

Roughly 800 American missiles have been used in just three days of fighting in the Middle East. More than Ukraine has fired during their entire war.

If this doesn't show you the severity, and URGENCY of this situation, we're not sure what will.

This is not a time to play politics, point fingers or sit around scratching our heads. American citizens are still stranded. Still waiting. Still hoping their government considers their lives worth the urgency.

The peop

🚨Severe Weather Warning🚨

Tonight into tomorrow, the Lowcountry is under a severe weather watch. Strong winds, heavy thunderstorms, and a chance of tornadoes are in the forecast.

Stay aware, stay safe, and keep an eye on your local weather updates.

South Carolina STRONG.

We still need planes to get Americans out.


Rep. Nancy Mace (@RepNancyMace)

We brought the attention. Washington listened. God provided.

More planes. More evacuations. More Americans coming home from the Middle East.

Thank you to the @StateDept, El Al Airlines and every embassy working tirelessly for our people.

This is what America does. We do not abandon our own. We do not look away. We do not wait. No man, woman, or child left behind.

@greybullrescue

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/

I do not want to send South Carolina’s sons and daughters into war with Iran.
Community note: Five days ago, Rep. Mace argued for continuation of the war in Iran, where soldiers from SC children are being sent: “President Trump is doing what needs to be done. . . . This is not the time for Congress to tie the hands of our Commander-in-Chief. We must finish the job.”

x.com/repnancymace/s…
